steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: We already know ∠A = ∠P = 52°. AA similarity requires just one more pair of equal angles. The right choice must supply information that produces a matching second angle pair in both triangles.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — AB = 30 and PQ = 30.: gives sides, not angles — can't establish AA from side lengths alone. ✗
  • Choice B — AB = 30 and QR = 90.: gives only one additional angle in one triangle — insufficient to match angle pairs. ✗
  • Choice C — The measures of angle B and angle R are 34° and 94°, respectively.: Triangle ABC gives ∠C = 180 − 52 − 34 = 94°; Triangle PQR gives ∠Q = 180 − 52 − 94 = 34°. All three pairs match: ∠A = ∠P, ∠B = ∠Q, ∠C = ∠R. ✓
  • Choice D — The measures of angle B and angle Q are 52° and 34°, respectively.: gives angles that don't produce matching pairs with the known 52°. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
